title: solanum tuberosum var. diemii
side_effects: Solanum tuberosum var. diemii can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds like glycoalkaloids. Ingestion of large amounts can also lead to headaches, dizziness, and in severe cases, respiratory failure. Excessive exposure may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and even blistering in some individuals. Additionally, improper preparation and consumption of the plant can trigger cardiovascular issues, including increased heart rate and blood pressure.
